violations.text: he door to the outside, located in the side exit in the dining room, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2025
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair on prep unit #1, #2, and prep unit #3. Refrigerator door gaskets shall be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2025

Staff failed to sanitize equipment and utensils after washing and rinsing them. After being cleaned, equipment and utensils must be sanitized in the 3-bay-sink or dishwasher. Employee was observed washing a pizza cutter in hand sink by prep units. Employee informed to clean and sanitize utensils and equipment at ware washing facilities only. Employee used 3-bay sink to clean and sanitize equipment. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/12/2025

Salad mix was observed held at 47 degrees Fahrenheit in the overhead salad refrigeration unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per kitchen staff, food items were loaded into unit less than 2 hours prior to inspection and were moved to walk-in cooler to rapidly cool. Cold hold unit observed held at an ambient temperature of 43 degrees Fahrenheit. Staff adjusted unit during time of inspection and unit was observed at 39 degrees Fahrenheit. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/12/2025

Cooked spicy chicken was observed held past its disposition date in the walk-in cooler. Foods items prepared within the establishment shall be discarded after 7 days which includes the date of preparation. Food items were disposed of during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/12/2025

Establishment is not maintaining daily time as a public health control logs. Per approval letter, using time as a public health control is contingent on maintaining daily time as a public health control logs with all required information including: time made, how many, and how many discarded after 3.5hrs. New Violation. Correct By: 05/12/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of staff, pasta was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep and covered with plastic wrap. Pasta was moved onto sheet pans to finish cooling process. Proper cooling methods reviewed during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/12/2025

Salads and desserts packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. New Violation. Correct By: 05/12/2025

The door to the outside, located in the side exit in the dining room, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2025
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair on prep unit #1, #2, and prep unit #3. Refrigerator door gaskets shall be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the pizza prep units and gaskets have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment and facilities shall be cleaned at a frequency necessary to prevent the accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 05/12/2025
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair on units throughout the establishment. Establishment has had machines serviced and gaskets are on order. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2024
